mappings.rationaleAI: Custer's Last Stand covers the Lakota, Northern Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors of the Plains, the context of westward expansion, and Native American resistance, directly addressing peoples on the Plains.
Genghis Khan covers the unification of Mongol tribes and the Pax Mongolica facilitating Silk Road trade across Central Asia, directly addressing peoples and cultures of Central Asia.

The Almoravid Empire covers the Berber dynasty ruling northwest Africa and Islamic Spain, the Sanhaja Berbers, the gold trade, and the conquest of Ghana, directly addressing peoples of the Maghrib and Mediterranean.

The Ramayana covers the great Sanskrit epic's enormous cultural influence across South and Southeast Asia, Hindu devotion, and its multiple versions across the subcontinent, directly addressing peoples and cultures of South Asia.

Cook's three Pacific voyages mapped New Zealand and eastern Australia, circumnavigated Antarctica, and encountered indigenous peoples across Polynesia and Melanesia, directly addressing peoples of Oceania.

The Arab conquests cover the rapid spread of Islam across the Middle East and North Africa, the conquest of the Sassanid Persian Empire and Byzantine territories, directly addressing peoples of the Mashriq and Maghrib.
The Battle of Talas between the Abbasid Caliphate and Tang Dynasty in Central Asia, with Turkic allies, directly addresses the peoples and cultures of Central Asia and their interactions.
The Ming Voyages across Southeast Asia, India, the Persian Gulf, and East Africa under Zheng He directly address peoples and cultures of East Asia and their interactions with other regions.

The Phoenicians as an ancient Canaanite maritime civilization centered in modern Lebanon, serving as cultural intermediaries between the ancient Near East and the Greek world, directly address peoples of the Mashriq.

Bolivar's liberation campaigns across Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Bolivia and the social and racial complexities of Latin American independence directly address peoples and cultures of the central and southern Andes and South America.

The Byzantine Empire spanning Constantinople through the eastern Mediterranean, its relations with the Islamic world, and its cultural and intellectual legacy address peoples along the Mediterranean and in Turkey.

Alexander the Great's conquests from Greece through Persia to Central Asia and India, the founding of cities including Alexandria, and the Hellenistic cultural fusion address peoples along the Mediterranean, in the Mashriq, and in Central Asia.

The Silk Road's network connecting China with the Mediterranean through Central Asia, covering Sogdians, Parthians, Kushans, and the spread of Buddhism and Islam, directly addresses peoples across Central Asia, East Asia, South Asia, and the Mashriq.
The Tale of Sinuhe covers a court official's exile among Asiatic peoples during the Egyptian Middle Kingdom, directly addressing peoples of the Mashriq in the ancient Near East.
The Epic of Gilgamesh as the foundational work of ancient Mesopotamian civilization directly addresses peoples and cultures of the Mashriq region.

Homo erectus covers the dispersal of this hominin species from Africa across Asia and Europe, its adaptation to diverse environments, directly relevant to the earliest human populations across multiple world regions.

Strabo's Geographica provides a comprehensive description of peoples, customs, and landscapes across the Roman Empire and beyond, directly addressing peoples along the Mediterranean and in the Mashriq.
